decision_rationale: no_per_pass_fps_evidence: the GRX contract requires a per-pass FPS >= 0.95x baseline measured_local comparison before any default enablement (GRX_PLAN GRX.4 exit criteria / GRX_CONTRACT G-GRX-5 single_scene_fps_ratio_min=0.95), and no full baseline or per-pass benchmark comparison for fused_post_chain exists yet

patch_0038_writeback_is_scaffold: the fused kernel is dispatched into the LDR tonemap destination (dst_color) and the scaffold dst_luminance (also the LDR dest, fully overwritten) while the native Godot luminance_reduction + tonemap chain continuation re-renders every frame as the backstop, so the candidate image cannot change (LDR visual gate max_abs=0/mean_abs=0) and the opt-in Rurix dispatch yields no measured net benefit yet

scaffold_fused_binding: the fused luminance segment's distinct <=8x8 reduce level and double-buffered 1x1 prev/dst luminance targets are NOT exposed by the public Luminance API at the tonemap call site, so patch 0037 aliases the single public 1x1 current luminance buffer to lum_source and prev_luminance (read-only) and the LDR tonemap destination to dst_color and dst_luminance; the strict success asserts ONLY that a real D3D12 fused dispatch was recorded and the native-continuation visual gate stayed bit-stable, NOT that the fused output is correct or a net win. A distinct, correct fused binding (with the auto-exposure-on, glow-off, LINEAR, SDR fusion precondition truly satisfied) needs the deferred Luminance-API extension / draw_graph route B (PASS_CONTRACT.md 3.4/5)

linear_sdr_subset_only: math parity covers only the fused EMA-x-tonemap (TONEMAPPER_LINEAR + linear_to_srgb) subset (math_parity_evidence.json status=pending_gpu_dispatch); glow, tonemapper modes other than LINEAR (Reinhard/FILMIC/ACES/AgX), the rest of the auto-exposure consumer chain, and the same-frame tonemap replacement (one-frame latency) are recorded gaps
does_not_imply: math-correctness of the fused output (the lum_source/prev/dst bindings are a scaffold; the strict success asserts only that a real dispatch was recorded and the native-continuation visual gate stayed bit-stable)

broader fused_post_chain coverage (glow, non-LINEAR tonemappers, the rest of the auto-exposure consumer chain, same-frame tonemap replacement) beyond the LINEAR + linear_to_srgb + auto-exposure subset
net-benefit Rurix dispatch (patch 0038 result writeback is a scaffold; the native Godot luminance_reduction + tonemap chain re-renders every frame)

reasons: bench_7_scenes_never_engage: LINEAR-tonemap AND auto-exposure luminance buffers are mutually exclusive across the seven bench scenes (LINEAR∩AE=∅); rd_native_final_20260713 records fused 0/7 non-engagement, all5_fused is an A/A control only

ae_scene_parity_out_of_tolerance: on the dedicated CameraAttributesPractical AE enablement scene the fused kernel genuinely engages but its LDR output diverges from native auto-exposure by max_abs=85/mean_abs=66 (thresholds max<=4/mean<=1.0) because the b0 auto-exposure scalars are placeholders pending the Luminance-API extension; status=measured_engaged_parity_out_of_tolerance, real_gpu_pass=false, first_missing_prerequisite=fused_tonemap_parity_out_of_tolerance

structural_net_zero: the luminance-final write is a shadow recompute to a self-owned scratch never read back, the native luminance_reduction pyramid still runs every frame, net_dispatch_saving=0, structural_fusion_claimed=false; a genuine fusion (Design 2) is a later batch
rd_native_default_enable.cascade_confirmed: fused -> tonemap rd_native -> native two-level fallback measured byte-identical on the AE-off cascade leg
